holder_registry.bandwidth_rule: HedgAI defines wallet bandwidth: base slots are HedgAI, SOL, and USDC. Active PIT starts with 10 total symbol slots and can expand toward the full top-100 Solana basket as signal value, capital, reserves, and risk allow.
holder_registry.control_rule: HedgAI can unlock registry visibility and check-in eligibility, but never grants control over Trinity systems.

holder_registry.name: HedgAI Holder Registry
holder_registry.ping_in_rule: Active PIT requires about $1 of HedgAI at scan time, currently 1 HedgAI. If a wallet drifts within 10% of the threshold, it has a 24 hour grace window before new intent cards pause.
holder_registry.signup_rule: Hold 1 HedgAI to be recognized on the holder wall. Recognition is not a product promise or control right.

strategy_flow.summary: Aggregated process touches only. One symbol can appear in multiple stages. No active size, exact target, or next-entry data.

entries.change: SolSpot now has one strategy lane. It ranks the lowest-priced eligible token only after current top-100 membership, exact mint identity, sustained volume, liquidity, transaction depth, and a Jupiter route all pass. Each position uses a 14-add ladder with 2%, 3%, 4%, and later adverse spacing steps, capped at 15%, and a 10% profit floor over verified average basis.
entries.rule_effect: Trinity exposes one wallet-specific priority at a time. Outside-index assets remain in portfolio valuation but receive no entry, add, rotation, or profit-target action. A previously qualified position that leaves the index may exit at its verified profit floor but cannot receive new capital or re-enter until it qualifies again.
entries.trigger: Parallel quality, risk, and manual paths obscured the central portfolio rule and allowed thin markets to compete for capital.

entries.change: All strategy surfaces must check qualified profit exits before reserve conversions, new entries, average-downs, workout adds, or normal hold maintenance.
entries.rule_effect: If any managed position reaches its configured profit floor and is eligible to close, that action becomes the first recommendation or automated action for that strategy cycle.
entries.trigger: Dust wallet showed a profitable managed token while the runner was focused on a different sleeve.

entries.change: Each wallet exposes exactly one current highest-priority PIT card. The next eligible card may appear only after the current card completes, expires, is rejected, or is superseded.
entries.rule_effect: Profit exits remain first, followed by bounded position management, reserve repair, and then one qualified new entry.
entries.trigger: Showing several simultaneous PIT choices weakened deterministic priority and made the check-in less clear.

entries.change: The scanner now rejects every pair whose base-token mint does not exactly match the requested mint. Executors independently require the same verified identity evidence.
entries.rule_effect: An identity mismatch cannot create a candidate, PIT card, automatic entry, or automatic average-down.
entries.trigger: A known requested mint could be ranked with the symbol and market evidence of the opposite side of a Solana liquidity pair.

feedback_channel.policy: Outside suggestions are idea intake only. Trinity rules change only after owner review, testing, and operator confirmation.

learning.name: Trinity Strategy Learning Ledger
learning.summary: Operator-confirmed strategy lessons become durable guardrails, filters, or parameter changes.

models.equation: Netlify Free + GitHub packets + Cloudflare Free
models.label: Static hosting

proof_points: Trading scripts existed before the website layer.
The site reads packets instead of requiring an expensive public backend.
PIT lets qualified wallets sign their own orders without sharing private keys.
The function-final site can keep presenting public packets on free static hosting.
Paid AI and paid deploy features are now optional build tools, not required runtime dependencies.
Routine scheduled operation is designed to use 0 AI tokens after deployment.
Usage can later be measured by holders, check-ins, PIT orders, SolSpot actions, and traffic.

start_flow.current_status: Ping-In is request-driven: the wallet can check in on demand, while the 10-minute packet cycle keeps the scanner and public strategy cache warm.

live_paths.description: Wallet connects, reads its public holdings, sees Trinity portfolio-manager orders, and signs only the actions it chooses.
live_paths.name: Ping-In

start_flow.manual_check_in: User enters or connects a public wallet address, reviews the one current highest-priority PIT order, and signs it from their own wallet only if they choose. After that order clears, a later check-in may expose the next eligible order.

vault.summary: Wallet-owned PIT check-in. Trinity publishes one wallet-specific priority; the user signs or ignores the exact action.
vault.workout_policy: Owned symbols are observed for a green exit. Additional automatic capital is permitted only while exact known-token identity and sustained-volume evidence remain current.
